description:
Verified correct in this pass:

1. HashcheckTicket construction: TPM2_ST_HASHCHECK tag, TPM2_RH_NULL hierarchy, empty digest — all map correctly through tss-esapi's TryFrom chain (StructureTag::Hashcheck, Hierarchy::Null). Correct per TPM 2.0 spec Part 3 section 11.4.1 for unrestricted signing keys.

2. SubjectPublicKeyInfo::try_from(Public) does NOT require an abstraction feature gate. In tss-esapi 7.7.0, picky-asn1-x509 is a hard (non-optional) dependency and the abstraction module is unconditionally compiled. No cfg guard needed.

3. NvAuth::Owner is correct for the documented model. The code and docs together correctly constrain to owner-hierarchy-authorized NV indices with empty auth.

4. normalize_signature properly zero-pads ECDSA r/s to 32 bytes using saturating_sub, handling short TPM outputs correctly. The AlgorithmMismatch arm catches ECC-when-RSA-expected and vice versa at sign() time.

5. Mode guards on get/put/sign/public_key_der/algorithm consistently return InvalidUri, preventing silent misuse of NV handles as keys or vice versa.

6. map_tpm_error correctly classifies Retry, Yielded, NvRate, NvUnavailable, Memory, etc. as transient (Unavailable) and everything else as permanent (Backend). Good granularity.

7. Context-per-operation model is correct — tss-esapi Context is !Send so it cannot be held across await points. Creating fresh contexts in spawn_blocking is the right pattern.

8. Inventory registrations correctly wire NV URIs to SecretStore+WritableSecretStore and key URIs to SigningBackend, with cross-mode rejection at factory time.
title: Review pass 3: what is good
